channel.title: World In 5
channel.description: The world's top news stories in 5 minutes, every morning. Politics, international affairs, climate, and the events shaping our world â€” clear, fast, free.
channel.itunes:author: World In 5

item.title: World News Jun 29 — Pakistani strikes kill dozens in Afghanistan
item.description: Dozens of civilians are dead and many more are injured in Afghanistan after Pakistani airstrikes ripped through the border region, sparking outrage and escalating tensions between the two nations. The Taliban government claims that the strikes targeted innocent people, including women and children, while Pakistan insists that it was targeting militant groups. This is a critical moment in the already fragile relationship between Pakistan and Afghanistan, with both sides accusing each other of vio...

item.title: World In 5 | Jun 30 — Police units deployed across South Africa before a
item.description: Tensions are running high in South Africa today as police units have been deployed across the country in anticipation of anti-immigration marches, sparking fears of a repeat of the deadly violence that rocked the nation back in 2008. The government is taking no chances, with security personnel patrolling the streets of major cities like Johannesburg, where many shopkeepers have opted to keep their businesses closed today. The planned demonstrations are aimed at undocumented foreign nationals, an...

item.title: World In 5 | Jun 30 — Police units deployed across South Africa before a
item.description: Racial tensions are simmering in South Africa, where police units have been deployed across the country ahead of anti-immigration marches, sparking fears of a repeat of the deadly violence that erupted in 2008. The authorities are taking no chances, with security personnel patrolling the streets of major cities like Johannesburg, where many shopkeepers have chosen to stay closed today. The marches are planned in response to the presence of undocumented foreign nationals, and the government is ke...

item.title: World In 5 | Jul 01 — Police units deployed across South Africa before a
item.description: Riots, earthquakes, and a president's crypto windfall - it's a wild day in global news, and we're just getting started. In South Africa, police units are flooding the streets ahead of planned anti-immigration marches, and the government is bracing for the worst. You might remember the devastating anti-migrant violence back in 2008, which left 62 people dead and a trail of destruction in its wake. Authorities are determined to avoid a repeat of that chaos, deploying security personnel to key area...

item.title: World In 5 | Jul 02 — Trump refuses to renew US-Canada-Mexico trade pact
item.description: In a stunning U-turn, former President Donald Trump has refused to renew the US-Canada-Mexico trade pact, a deal he once touted as one of his greatest achievements. The United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement, or USMCA, was set to expire in 2036, but Wednesday was the deadline for the three countries to jointly decide its fate. Instead of committing to another 16 years, Trump and US officials have opted to keep the pact alive on a short leash of annual reviews. This move is significant because it ...

item.title: World In 5 | Jul 03 — ‘Give him any award, and he’ll come running’: Nare
item.description: You've got to wonder, what's the real cost of a trophy case full of international awards for a head of state, and is Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi's collection of honors on his travels a symbol of diplomatic strength or just a penchant for prestige. Modi's habit of racking up awards on his overseas trips has raised eyebrows, with some questioning the legitimacy of these honors, given that some of them are being awarded to him as their first and only recipient. The latest addition to his co...

item.title: World In 5 | Jul 04 — New pipeline in Canada to proceed after C$150bn pl
item.description: In a move that's set to send shockwaves through the global energy landscape, Canada has just greenlit a massive new oil pipeline, thanks to a whopping C$150 billion pledge aimed at silencing concerns from British Columbia and First Nations groups. This is huge, because the project promises to boost Canada's presence on the world stage, while also addressing some of the environmental and social worries that had been threatening to derail it. The plan includes a major port expansion and measures t...
